feedback.text: Hello. I kindly ask to please add a further protection to the Digital Fairness Act, protecting paying video game customers from developer/publisher companies that make certain video games with online features unplayable whenever they just want. Hundreds or maybe thousands of games are affected, and the list of these games are on the internet, you can look it up. Currently it is unfortunately legal and a common practice for a company to sell a game which requires internet connection and then decommission the game server anytime they want, making the game unplayable, and not compensating the customer who paid for the game. I suggest making it mandatory for the companies at the time of decommissioning to provide the server software for the customers so they can decide if they want to operate community servers on their own budget and time, and therefore keep the game playable. There are many gamers like me who want a new regulation. More than 1 million EU citizens signed the European Citizens' Initiative titled Stop Destroying Videogames, Commission registration number: ECI(2024)000007. Thank you.
